mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns show no statistical significance during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.5 percent, labor market deterioration signals imminent corporate earnings declines, driving negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day forward returns occur in more than 55 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital demand growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a negative Sharpe ratio over 60-day horizons or fails to outperform a single-proxy baseline in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during periods of stable inflation reflects robust supply-side capacity utilization, which drives positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses or becomes statistically insignificant when CPIAUCSL month-over-month growth exceeds 0.4 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp monthly inflation spikes trigger expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns remain positive in over 60 percent of high-inflation months or Rank IC stays below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms broad-based labor market strength, supporting corporate revenue growth and positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal produces negative 60-day returns in more than half of observed periods or shows zero predictive power in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product growth directly increases aggregate corporate earnings expectations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth shows no correlation with 20-day forward returns or yields a negative information coefficient over rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Consecutive monthly declines in industrial production signal deteriorating manufacturing activity and weakening economic momentum, driving negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO crosses below its 3-month moving average
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in more than 50 percent of consecutive decline periods or the signal loses predictive power during recessionary phases.
